ops.body: Phase-3 live run: design gap found and routed. The shipped serve lane has NO volume-population step — serve_modal.py mounts the adapter volume but nothing fills it, the spike's upload_adapter.py is lost (absent from .spike-reference/ and /tmp), and the spike-era Modal volumes were deleted. The live-ops agent stopped correctly instead of hand-rolling an MLX→PEFT reverse-converter (subtly-wrong-adapter failure mode). Resolution: no converter should ever exist — the promoted version's metadata durably addresses the ORIGINAL PEFT bytes (tinker_checkpoint tinker://03d33d17-…/sampler_weights/watcher-sft-590); the live run re-fetches them read-only (~cents) via athome's own checkpoint-download machinery, format-verifies, and batch-uploads. DESIGN NOTE FOR PHASE 4 (the reviewed fix): the hosted lane consumes PEFT bytes at the source — the retrain/promote path preserves or re-fetches the pre-conversion PEFT artifact (address already in registry metadata) and a serve-side uploader (e.g. serve_modal.upload_adapter) lands with the 9B work; reverse conversion from MLX is permanently off the table.
